People behind DeepPhe

Team

DeepPhe is developed by a multi-institutional team of researchers, clinicians, informaticians, and software developers working to make information in cancer clinical notes more useful for research and cancer registry workflows.

Project leadership

Guergana K. Savova, PhD

Principal Investigator

Boston Children's Hospital and Harvard Medical School

View profile →

Harry Hochheiser, PhD

Site Principal Investigator

University of Pittsburgh Department of Biomedical Informatics

View profile →

Jeremy L. Warner, MD, MS

Site Principal Investigator

Brown University

Medical Oncologist and Hematologist

View profile →

Technical leadership

SF

Sean Finan

Principal Engineer

Boston Children's Hospital

View profile →

Institutions and collaboration

The team members listed here are affiliated with Boston Children's Hospital, Harvard Medical School, the University of Pittsburgh, and Brown University. DeepPhe-CR was developed in partnership with the Kentucky Cancer Registry.

Funding and support

Work on DeepPhe Translational and DeepPhe Visualizer was supported by the National Cancer Institute's Information Technology for Cancer Research program under grant U24CA248010. Work on DeepPhe-CR was supported by NCI's Surveillance, Epidemiology, and End Results program under grant UH3CA243120.
